title: Field-Relative Undercut And Traffic Opportunity Features
summary: Pit timing depends on competition, not just tyre age.
rationale: Pit timing depends on competition, not just tyre age. Strategy references and recent operations-research papers emphasize traffic, undercut payoff, and opponent interaction; ignoring competition can materially degrade decisions.
implementation_hint: Within each race-lap, build relative features: gap to nearby cars, percentile of current lap time within field, compound/tyre-life contrast versus cars ahead/behind, and an approximate undercut score using expected fresh-tyre gain minus pit-loss and traffic penalty proxies. Keep all features strictly contemporaneous within the same lap snapshot.
expected_effect: Should reduce false positives where tyres are old but traffic makes stopping unattractive, and surface true positives where an undercut window opens despite moderate degradation.
risk: Same-lap field features are safe only if they use information observable by that lap; any use of next-lap positions, finalized gaps, or post-stop outcomes will leak.
